title: Ruby Source
documentation:
## Purpose
Ruby source files contain code written in Ruby, a dynamic, object-oriented scripting language known for its expressive syntax and the Rails web framework. Ruby is used for web development, scripting, build automation (Rake), and tool development.

## When to use
Use [Ruby Source](https://capdag.com/browse/media:ruby;textable;code) for `.rb` application files, `Rakefile` and `.rake` task files, and `.gemspec` gem specification files. All are syntactically valid Ruby and can be processed by the same toolchain.

## Notes
Ruby source is UTF-8 text. The language is highly dynamic with pervasive metaprogramming via `method_missing`, `define_method`, and open classes, which limits static analysis accuracy. Ruby version management (rbenv, rvm, asdf) is common in Ruby projects; the required version is typically declared in `.ruby-version` or the `Gemfile`.
